method_atom: A systematic asymptotic (perturbative) expansion of a many-component system in the reciprocal of a large parameter N that counts internal degrees of freedom, in which the leading order becomes exactly solvable by saddle-point/mean-field techniques (including external-field solutions and determinant identities), and a joint 'double-scaling' limit — tuning N together with a coupling toward a critical point — extracts universal subleading behavior. It is complemented by strong-coupling series expansions, dimensionally-reduced reformulations, and stochastic (Monte Carlo) sampling of the underlying integrals.
object_atom: Statistical-mechanics and quantum-field-theory models built from group-valued (unitary-matrix) variables carrying an SU(N)×SU(N) symmetry — spin and gauge lattice models, the single-link integral (equivalent to two-dimensional Yang–Mills and one-dimensional chiral chains), closed chiral chains and models on a (d−1)-dimensional simplex, and the asymptotically free two-dimensional chiral models and four-dimensional QCD.
brief_reason: The 1/N large-parameter expansion with a saddle-point leading order and a double-scaling critical limit is the transferable approximation machinery, while the specific unitary-matrix / SU(N)×SU(N) spin and gauge field theories are the physics-specific objects it is applied to.
